step_by_step_thinking:
Tool Call: retrieve("is ADP-ribosylation a post-translational modification according to current scientific literature")

Retrieved Documents: 32 documents

After reviewing the provided evidence, I observe that multiple references explicitly identify ADP-ribosylation as part of the set of post-translational modifications affecting histone proteins. Specifically, source [1] lists "additional post-translational modifications to histone proteins" including ubiquitination, phosphorylation, and ADP-ribosylation—all alongside acetylation and methylation—as regulators of gene expression. Furthermore, source [17] discusses PARP inhibitors' suppression of DNA repair mechanisms via interference with poly(ADP-ribose) polymerase—an enzyme responsible for adding ADP-ribose units during ADP-ribosylation. Since this enzymatically regulated modification directly influences chromatin dynamics and DNA integrity, consistent with classic PTM behavior, the evidence strongly supports its classification as a bona fide PTM.
I therefore conclude that ADP-ribosylation meets the accepted criteria for a post-translational modification—not merely as a biochemical curiosity—but as a dynamically regulated event integral to epigenetic control and disease pathology.
\boxed{A}
Your task now ends here.
Please ensure your output adheres strictly to the instructions above: maximum two sentences, clear agreement/disagreement statement followed immediately by boxed letter choice.
